description_long: This is a single-story bungalow with a prominent red hip roof and a sprawling rectangular footprint. The exterior walls are finished in a combination of tan plaster and decorative brickwork, giving the building a warm, inviting appearance. Large windows are symmetrically arranged along the front and sides, allowing ample natural light into the interior. A covered porch with railings extends across the front facade, providing a welcoming entryway. The structure sits on a flat, paved base, typical of suburban or urban residential lots. The overall design is practical and emphasizes comfort, with architectural details that suggest a modern or contemporary bungalow style.
description: A single-story bungalow with a red hip roof, tan and brick exterior, and a covered front porch with symmetrical windows.
description_view_0: Front angled view showing the main entrance, covered porch, and prominent red hip roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long brick wall, multiple windows, and the depth of the structure.
description_view_2: Direct front view focusing on the porch, main entrance, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_3: Rear angled view showing the back and side windows, and the extended base platform.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with grouped side windows
